programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Minnie Red Riding Hood
programme.desc: Just like the fairy tale, Minnie comes across trouble in the woods.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Daisy's Pet Project
programme.desc: Daisy ends up with three pets for the Pet Parade, a baby elephant, giraffe and a bunny.

programme.title: Bluey: Trampoline/ The Dump/ Zoo
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo want to play games on the trampoline with Dad; Dad, Bluey and Bingo head to the dump to throw away their rubbish; Bluey and Bingo play pretend zoo.

programme.title: Bluey: Fairies/ Work/ Bumpy and the Wise Old Wolfhound
programme.desc: Bluey thinks there are Fairies in the backyard; Bluey and Bingo are hired by Dad at the drainpipe factory; Dad and Bluey send Bingo a video.

programme.title: Dino Ranch: Home on the Range/ Miguel's Compy Conundrum
programme.desc: The Cassidy family lives in a world with dinosaurs.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: The French Toast Connection/ Take Me Out to the Pug Game
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly travel to France to get Bob some French toast. Then, the pugs chase Bob's prized baseball across the city.

programme.title: Bluey: Muffin Cone/ Circus/ Road Trip
programme.desc: Muffin has to wear a cone of shame! Bluey is the ringmaster in a game of circus! The Heelers go on a road trip!

programme.title: Dino Ranch: Clover's Tiny Problem/ Tango's BFF
programme.desc: Clover realizes Min won't replace him with any dinosaur. Then, Tango becomes attached to a triceratops and doesn't want her to rejoin her herd.

programme.title: PJ Masks: Catboy No More/ Gekko Vs. Splatcano
programme.desc: After Romeo steals Catboy's powers, Catboy must learn to be a superhero without any super powers.

programme.title: PJ Masks: Pharaoh and the Ninjalinos/ Pharaoh's Boomerangs
programme.desc: When Night Ninja needs the PJs' help, our heroes can't help taking advantage of it. Pharaoh Boy absorbs the PJ's powers into his boomerangs.

programme.title: Bluey: The Show/ Ticklecrabs/ Escape
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey put on a Mother's Day show! The kids play Tickle Crabs with Dad! Bluey and Bingo get dropped off at Nana's.

programme.title: Bluey: The Quiet Game/ Postman/ Cafe
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo play The Quiet Game. When Mum and Dad have a disagreement, Bluey makes Dad write a love letter to Mum. In the park, Bluey meets a new playmate, Winnie.

programme.title: Doc McStuffins: A Big Pain in Teddy's Tummy/ Slip 'N Slide
programme.desc: While Donny plays with Teddy B and some other toys in the garden, Teddy B pokes Donny! It's a hot summer's day and Doc is at the park playing in the wading pool with her toys.

programme.title: Doc McStuffins: Dad's Favorite Toy/ Chilly and the Dude
programme.desc: During spring-cleaning, Dad, Donny and Doc find Dad's favorite toy, Saltwater Serge and Wellington Whale, in the attic; Doc and her Mom are putting together a winter charity basket, which includes a stuffed snowman that looks like Chilly.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: How ARF Got His Bark Back / Sir Bob
programme.desc: When Bob can't fix ARF's voice box, Bingo and Rolly race to find an inventor. Bob's favorite knight goes missing at a jousting event, plunging the Pugs on a mission to find him!

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Fantastic Pet Force / Sea No Turtle
programme.desc: The puppies leap into action to assemble their own real-life team of puppy heroes. Bob takes the pugs on a conservation vacation where he volunteers to study sea turtles.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: The French Toast Connection
programme.desc: Two puppies who love to have fun take on adventures around their neighbourhood.

programme.title: Bluey: Kids/ Butterflies/ The Creek
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey want to play a game during a shopping trip; Bluey runs away, leaving Bingo all alone; The kids and Dad go on an outing to the creek.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Good Luck Mr. Whiskers/ Who's Your Daddy
programme.desc: Theodore is convinced that Miss Croner's missing cat is the cause of a terrible curse. When Dave comes down with a bad cold, Alvin quickly offers to take over Dad duty.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Munk Man/ Ride Along
programme.desc: Simon creates a Super Hero costume for a convention he's attending. When Alvin's bicycle is stolen, he joins forces with Officer Dangus to solve the case.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Granny Awards/ Sick Day
programme.desc: Dave is thrilled when he thinks he's nominated for a Grammy, but it's actually an award for aging musicians. Alvin and Kevin pretend to be sick to miss school and sneak off to the Dagarack Convention.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Mojo Missing/ Who's the Animal
programme.desc: Alvin is convinced his mojo was in a cap he gave to a fan, and must find a way to get his hat back before the Chipmunks' big concert. During a costume visit to the zoo, Alvin finds himself mistaken for the real Tasmanian devil.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Suck Toad/ Secret Admirer
programme.desc: Brittany is preparing a class speech on 'inner beauty' when an endangered species makes its home on her face. Alvin believes that Kevin has a secret crush on one of the Chipettes.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Dr. Zap vs Electroboy/ Animal House Party
programme.desc: Theodore is hit by lightning and believes he's like his comic book hero, until he's told he's actually a villain. When Derek overhears that Alvin and his brothers are alone for the weekend, he plans a wild party.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Escape From Unwish Island/ The Gland Plan
programme.desc: Timmy's unwished wishes come back for revenge; Cosmo might lose his job as godparent.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: The Masked Magician/ The Big Bash
programme.desc: Timmy wishes for Magician powers to perform at the fair; Cupid has a party at his house.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Fool's Gold/ Flight Plan
programme.desc: When they find a treasure map, the Casagrandes go on a wild search for Pancho Villa’s gold./ Frida and Carlos go on a fancy plane ride, but Carl, Cj and Carlitos try to sneak on for an adventure of their own.

programme.title: The Casagrandes
programme.desc: A multi-generational Mexican-American family run a market in a big city.

programme.title: The Loud House: Singled Out/ Brave the Last Dance
programme.desc: Feeling like a third wheel when her teammates find significant others, Lynn seeks a boyfriend just to fit in. Clyde recruits Lincoln and the gang to find out if a girl he has a crush on at school feels the same way.

programme.title: The Loud House: A Star is Scorned/ Senior Moment
programme.desc: When Lola realizes that double the cuteness could help her break into show business, she teams up with Lily. Lori regrets everything she hasn't done in high school, and vows to make up for lost time.

programme.title: The Loud House: Patching Things Up/ Cheater by the Dozen
programme.desc: Lola and Lana try out for Bluebell Scouts but discover one of them might be more suited for it. When Lincoln and Clyde think Bobby is cheating on Lori they follow him around town gathering evidence.

programme.title: The Loud House: No Such Luck/ Frog Wild
programme.desc: The sisters think Lincoln is bad luck so they ban him from attending their activities. When Lincoln finds out his class is dissecting frogs, he and Lana embark on a mission to rescue them.

programme.title: Nick News: Kids, Immigration, and Equality
programme.desc: Children who have made an impact on real world issues are profiled.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Tooth, the Whole Tooth and Nothing but the Tooth/ Mutiny on the Krusty Krab
programme.desc: Krabs leads the Krusty Krab's crew and customers back home when a storm sweeps the Krusty Krab away. Patrick is afraid to let go of his last baby tooth.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Shellback Shenanigans/ Hide and Then What Happens?
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick play hide n' seek and SpongeBob searches the world for Patrick. Plankton masquerades as Gary to steal the Krabby Patty formula.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: House Sittin' for Sandy/ Smoothe Jazz at Bikini Bottom
programme.desc: All is quiet while SpongeBob is house-sitting for Sandy - until Patrick shows up.

programme.title: It's Pony: Gerry's Birthday/ Haircut
programme.desc: When Annie and Pony try to give Gerry the best birthday ever, their enthusiasm threatens to make it the worst. When Annie needs a haircut, Pony proves that even a simple task can become a wild adventure.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Tutor Sauce/ Lost in Bikini Bottom
programme.desc: SpongeBob tries to take a shortcut to work, but gets lost along the way. After failing his driver's test, Mr. Krabs decides to teach SpongeBob how to drive himself.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: SpongeBob LongPants/ Larry's Gym
programme.desc: SpongeBob gets a pair of long pants, but they change his life in unexpected ways.When Larry the Lobster opens a new gym in Bikini Bottom, SpongeBob becomes his best customer.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Mooncation
programme.desc: Sandy is off on a vacation to the moon and accidentally brings along SpongeBob.

programme.title: Breadwinners: Lil Loafie/ Oonski the Grateful
programme.desc: When T-Midi rejects a loaf of defective bread, SwaySway and Buhdeuce take the loaf under their wing and it becomes super famous. When the ducks accidentally save Oonski from a monster, he owes them a debt of gratitude.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Keep Bikini Bottom Beautiful/ A Pal for Gary
programme.desc: Squidward cleans up the city of Bikini Bottom with 'help' from SpongeBob. SpongeBob brings Gary a pet that terrorizes him.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Blackened Sponge/ Mermaidman vs. Spongebob
programme.desc: SpongeBob punches himself in the face, but tells everyone that he fought off a thug.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Chimps Ahoy/ Ghost House
programme.desc: The Chimpanzee beneficiaries of Sandy's undersea tree dome are paying a visit to see what progress she has made with her inventions. The Flying Dutchman takes up residence at SpongeBob's house.

programme.title: The Loud House: Lock 'N Loud/ The Whole Picture
programme.desc: Dad gets upset with the kids for never locking the front door; Lincoln accidentally erases his childhood photos.

programme.title: The Loud House: Cooked!
programme.desc: The kids promise to help Dad with the grand opening of his new restaurant by coming up with promotions. But their lack of teamwork and crazy promos lead to an ultimate nightmare in the kitchen for Dad.

programme.title: The Loud House: Singled Out/ Brave the Last Dance
programme.desc: Feeling like a third wheel when her teammates find significant others, Lynn seeks a boyfriend just to fit in. Clyde recruits Lincoln and the gang to find out if a girl he has a crush on at school feels the same way.

programme.title: The Loud House: On Thin Ice/ Room and Hoard
programme.desc: Lynn's sports superstitions cause chaos at a hockey match. Unable to part with their childhood toys, the kids try to convince Mom and Dad that they still play with them.

programme.title: The Loud House: Write and Wrong/ Purrfect Gig
programme.desc: Mom tries to convince a newspaper editor that her kids are perfect in order to land a parenting advice column job. When the McBrides go out of town, they hire Luna to catsit but the job becomes more demanding than she imagined.

programme.title: The Loud House: The Sweet Spot/ A Tale of Two Tables
programme.desc: Lincoln plots to obtain a prime seat for the family's roadtrip. Lincoln acts more mature in order to eat at the adult table.

programme.title: The Loud House: Fed Up/ Shell Shock
programme.desc: The kids make a plan to sabotage dinner so they can have pizza. Lincoln and Ronnie Anne have to take care of an egg baby, but Lincoln doesn't trust her with the fragile egg.

programme.title: The Loud House: Sitting Bull/ The Spies Who Loved Me
programme.desc: The older sisters reluctantly let Lynn into their babysitting club. Ronnie Anne wants to go downtown, but her grandparents think it's too dangerous.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Mustard O' Mine/ Shopping List
programme.desc: When The Krusty Krab runs out of mustard, Mr. Krabs sends SpongeBob, Patrick and Squidward to dig up some more. SpongeBob and Sandy have to collect Krabby Patty ingredients, but Plankton is following their every step.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Life Insurance/ Burst Your Bubble
programme.desc: After a misunderstanding, SpongeBob and Patrick try to prove their life insurance protects them from any danger. When SpongeBob blows himself a bubble boat, everyone in town wants one. Everyone except Mrs. Puff, that is.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Safety Third/ Mister Manners
programme.desc: When Dave refuses to let Alvin get a dangerous toy sports car, Alvin secretly convinces Simon to build him a replica of the car. Alvin must pass a manners course before he gets his dream meeting with the Princess of England.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Un-send!/ The Orb
programme.desc: Simon accidentally sends an email venting about a professor and the boys must keep the teacher from reading it! Theodore has a hard time saying no and trouble ensues when Alvin needs Theodore to say yes.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Chimpmania/ Dad Jokes
programme.desc: Jeanette and the Chipmunks orchestrate a break-in at Bocarter's house to rescue a chimpanzee he keeps in a cage. Alvin is alarmed that Dave will be hosting an auction where he'll have to tell jokes because he's not that funny.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Back to School/ Bromance
programme.desc: When Dave discovers he didn't graduate from high school, he finds himself in school with Alvin. Simon has found a new best friend, Jamie. But when Jamie realizes that Alvin is the more popular brother, he ditches Simon.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Missing Miss Smith/ Monster Madness
programme.desc: When Alvin finally pushes Miss Smith too far, she quits and her replacement is a nightmare. Alvin and Simon tease The Chipettes about being scared by a movie.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: The Chosen Chipmunk/ Double Dangus
programme.desc: Alvin sneaks into Simon's new virtual reality game and is transported to a world in desperate need of a hero. The chipmunks discover Officer Dangus has a twin brother.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Just Desserts/ You Doo
programme.desc: After wishing that all the food in the world were desserts, Timmy helps himself to all the sweets. There is a magic doll that only fairies can make which can give you the power to make other people do whatever you tell them.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Open Wide and Say Aaahg
programme.desc: Timmy's tonsils are coming out and nurse Vicky is determined to make his visit unpleasant.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Remy Rides Again/ Hassle in the Castle
programme.desc: Timmy slips into Cosmo and Wanda's fish castle; Remy fools Timmy into being his friend.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Timmy the Barbarian/ No Substitute for Crazy
programme.desc: Jorgen Von Strangle tells the very macho story of muscular warrior Timmy the Barbarian.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Arr in the Family/ Finders Weepers
programme.desc: Ronnie Anne wants to help CJ to get onstage at a Pirate Dinner Show. Then, Ronnie and Sid decide whether to take money from a lost coin purse they found at the mercado.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Guess Who's Shopping for Dinner/ New Roomie
programme.desc: Abuela teaches Ronnie Anne that getting ingredients for dinner is no small task. When Vito's apartment floods, the kids try to find an apartment that suits him best.

programme.title: The Barbarian and the Troll: I Will Survive
programme.desc: The questers finally reach Alvin’s lair, but must first overcome a series of traps before Brendar can finally reunite with her brother.

programme.title: The Loud House: No Laughing Matter/ No Spoilers
programme.desc: When Luan overhears her siblings complaining about her comedy, she decides to give it up. Knowing Leni's history of spoiling surprises, the kids don't tell her about Mom's upcoming party.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Run, Candace, Run/ Last Train to Bustville
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb's newest invention is a pair of boots that make the wearer extraordinarily fast. Candace decides to borrow them so she can be in two places at once. Then, the kids decide to create and race a giant hot air balloon at their grandparents.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Double Dipper
programme.desc: When Stan throws a big weekend party to promote the Mystery Shack, Dipper uses an off-kilter copy machine to make clones of himself so he can impress Wendy. Meanwhile, Mabel stands up for her friends and fights for the Party Queen title in their honour.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Fight Fighters
programme.desc: After being challenged to a video game battle by Wendy's boyfriend Robbie, Dipper is shocked when his favourite playable fighter, Rumble McSkirmish, comes to life to defend him. Dipper soon loses control over Rumble and has to find a way to stop him.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Last Mabelcorn
programme.desc: A new threat leads Mabel to venture into the enchanted realm of the unicorns. Meanwhile, Dipper learns an unexpected twist about the enigmatic Bill Cipher.

programme.title: DuckTales: Day of the Only Child!
programme.desc: Dewey creates a holiday so that triplets can be only children for just one day. Quickly, the brothers learn that they need each other more than they realized.

programme.title: DuckTales: JAW$!
programme.desc: Lena unleashes a magical megalodon in Scrooge's money bin.

programme.title: DuckTales: The Golden Lagoon of White Agony Plains!
programme.desc: Scrooge reteams with his conniving ex-partner and old flame, Goldie O' Gilt, to find a long-lost golden lagoon, but Glomgold has other plans.

programme.title: Amphibia: Scavenger Hunt/ The Plantars Check In
programme.desc: Anne, Sprig and Marcy receive a mysterious message from the king of Newtopia that sends them on a puzzle-solving mission. Anne finally meets with King Andrias and tries to enlist his help in getting back home.

programme.title: Amphibia: Lost in Newtopia/ Sprig Gets Schooled
programme.desc: Anne and Polly vow to experience the city like locals but end up on a wild ride through the streets of Newtopia. Sprig is offered a spot at Newtopia University.

programme.title: Amphibia: Little Frogtown/ Hopping Mall
programme.desc: Hop Pop investigates the disappearance of an old friend and the shadowy crime syndicate he suspects are behind it. Hop Pop takes the kids to a Newtopian shopping mall to buy souvenirs, and Anne will do anything to get the perfect gift for her mom.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Attack of the 50 Foot Sister/ Backyard Aquarium
programme.desc: The boys create a PhineasandFerbalizer to speed up the growing process of vegetables.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Day of the Living Gelatin!/ Elementary, My Dear Stacy
programme.desc: Pool filled with bouncy gelatin and it comes to life; later family takes trip to England.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Don't Even Blink/ Chez Platypus
programme.desc: A determined Candace decides to find out where her brothers creations disappear to, and Phineas and Ferb open a platypus themed restaurant.

programme.title: Parker Plays
programme.desc: YouTube star Parker Coppins shares his hilarious gameplay commentary.

programme.title: Polaris: Player Select: Push, Pull, Pogo
programme.desc: Jack tries to deliver mail on a pogo stick, Markiplier takes on a boss battle in Cuphead, Dan swallows up scenery in Donut County, and Muselk attempts a new Fortnite challenge.

programme.title: Polaris: Player Select: Crown on the Ground
programme.desc: A compilation of top gaming videos from YouTube stars.

programme.title: Amphibia: Cane Crazy / Flood, Sweat & Tears
programme.desc: When Anne breaks Hop Pop's favorite cane, she and her friends set out to replace it; After Anne's bedroom floods she has to share a room with Sprig.

programme.title: Amphibia: Hop Luck / Stakeout
programme.desc: To win a village cooking contest, Anne leads the family on a quest to make pizza; Sprig tricks Anne and Hop Pop into going on a stakeout in order to make them get along.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Bottomless Pit!
programme.desc: When Stan, Dipper, Mabel and Soos find themselves falling into a bottomless pit, they each decide to tell a different story to pass the time.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Boyz Crazy
programme.desc: Mabel joins a secret knitting club and is pressured by her new friends to gossip, but when she tells them that Dipper likes Wendy, she inadvertently puts Dipper's life in danger.

programme.title: The Owl House: Agony of a Witch
programme.desc: On a school field trip to the mysterious Emperor's Castle, Luz strays from the group and into danger.

programme.title: The Owl House: Young Blood, Old Souls
programme.desc: Luz's skills as a witch are put to the test when she attempts the impossible.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Nerds of a Feather
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb attend a Comic Book Convention in Danville and they are excited to show some of their designs to one of their heroes.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Phineas and Ferb Hawaiian Vacation
programme.desc: During a Hawaiian family vacation, Candace finds a cool lava rock necklace and is plagued with bad luck, so a native tells her she must take it to the top of a volcano, which spoils the cool, calm vibe she learned from a Hawaiian yoga instructor.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Dungeons, Dungeons and More Dungeons
programme.desc: Dipper finds an unlikely friend to join him with his newest obsession, a board game called 'Dungeons, Dungeons & More Dungeons.'

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Stanchurian Candidate
programme.desc: When Grunkle Stan decides to run for mayor, Dipper and Mabel have their work cut out for them trying to turn their gaffe-prone uncle into the perfect candidate.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Space Chicken/ Steak Night
programme.desc: To impress his new neighbors, Cricket attempts to launch a chicken into space. The Greens are excited to continue their traditional monthly steak dinner, but Cricket accidentally loses the steaks on the subway.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Trailer Trouble/ Mansion Madness
programme.desc: Nancy's trailer is stolen by her old biker gang, and the kids resolve to get it back. Cricket house-sits for Remy, but things go awry when the rest of the Greens visit.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Date Night/ The Room
programme.desc: Bill chaperones Cricket and Gabriella's first date. Then, Tilly closes up Cricket's secret escape hole in their room, sparking a conflict that leads them to splitting the room down the middle.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Big Deal/ Forbidden Feline
programme.desc: Cricket persuades the family to hit up a Thanksgiving sale for a sweet deal on a new TV. Tilly hits it off with Anoosh, the neighborhood cat.

programme.title: Amphibia: Toad Tax/ Prison Break
programme.desc: Anne befriends toad soldiers to gain respect in Wartwood, but must choose between her new respect and her frog family. Far from Wartwood, Anne’s friend Sasha is captured by toads.

programme.title: Amphibia: Grubhog Day/ Hop Pop and Lock
programme.desc: During Grubhog Day, Sprig has to decide whether to uphold his responsibilities or have fun at the carnival. To impress Sylvia Sundew, Hop Pop asks Anne to teach him to dance.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Party Animal/ The Toddler
programme.desc: Sylvia intervenes when Emperor Awesome's partying threatens to destroy a planet.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Epic Quest of Unfathomable Difficulty/ The Void
programme.desc: Wander forces Sylvia to go on the most mind blowingly epic quest ever in order to return a lost sock to its owner. When Wander and Sylvia enter a white void where anything is possible through imagination, Sylvia worries Wander may never want to leave.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Funk/ The Enemies
programme.desc: Lord Hater is taught a lesson after he turns to bullying the universe just to make himself feel better. Then, Lord Hater and Sir Brad Starlight team to fight the one person they hate more than each other, Wander.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Liar/ The Stray
programme.desc: Wander guides a family of cute aliens through the perils of an active volcano, but Sylvia must provide the optimism needed to overcome the challenge. Later, Wander and Sylvia meet a cute, helpless creature and try to help it, only to find out it's a trap.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Date/The Buddies
programme.desc: When Lord Hater gets stood up on a date, Wander convinces Sylvia to endure a date with Hater in order save the planet from Hater’s wrath. Then, Lord Hater is forced to play the role of Wander’s friend when he is trapped on his own prison planet.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Gift II: The Giftening/ The Gift
programme.desc: Mysterious gifts start popping up on Lord Hater's ship, zombie-fying each recipient with gratitude. Hater and Peepers try to escape the creeping terror delivering these gifts.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Big Job/ The Helper
programme.desc: Sylvia is recruited by a rebel commando team to help steal Lord Hater's latest secret weapon. Then, on one strange day, Wander is unable to find anybody who actually needs his help.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Rider
programme.desc: When Sylvia's old partner, Rider, shows up, Wander wonders if the roguish Rider makes a better teammate for Sylvia than he does.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Greater Hater
programme.desc: Wander and Sylvia's world is turned upside down with the arrival of a new villain that threatens Lord Hater's status as the Greatest in the Galaxy.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Big Day/ The Breakfast
programme.desc: Lord Hater finally captures Wander and Sylvia, but before Wander meets his doom, he convinces Hater convinces Hater to plan a special event for the occasion. Wander and Hater each struggle in their own way when making breakfast.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Fremergency Fronfract / The Boy Wander
programme.desc: When Wander picks up a loopy Lord Hater from the dentist, he spends the day showing Hater how much fun being good can be. Then, Wander heroically faces a ridiculous villain from his past who no one takes seriously.

programme.title: Wander Over Yonder: The Wanders/ The Axe
programme.desc: Wander and Sylvia go from planet to planet helping people live free and have fun.

programme.title: Beyblade Burst Rise: Shining Bright! Hyper-Flux!
Phineas and Ferb: For Your Ice Only/ Happy New Year
programme.desc: NHL superstar Luc Robitaille offers his assistance when Phineas and Ferb take an ice hockey game to the extreme. Phineas and Ferb plan their own New Year's Eve ball drop, complete with a customized multi-dimensional ball, while Candice makes a resolution.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Bully Bust/ Backyard Hodge Podge
programme.desc: Candace challenges Buford to keep the boys' invention from disappearing.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Der Kinderlumper/ Just Desserts
programme.desc: Candace is chosen to be Rutabega princess and gets to start the Running of the Chinchillas.

programme.desc: Dan Le Batard provides his questionable approach to sports, highlighting his thoughtful, irreverent and often humorous perspective on the world of professional sports.

programme.title: Jalen & Jacoby
programme.desc: Jalen Rose and David Jacoby give the people what they want, breaking down the latest in sports and pop culture as only they can.

programme.desc: Host, Tony Reali, and sports reporters representing each time zone via satellite, banter about their local and national sports stories. Reali hosts and keeps score on every comment from his studio where he is connected to each columnist's newsroom.

programme.title: Great Performances at the Met: Anna Netrebko in Concert

programme.title: American Masters: Itzhak Perlman

programme.title: Independent Lens: Man on Fire
programme.desc: Learn the story of an elderly white Texas minister who set himself on fire to protest the virulent racism of his community and the country at large. Friends, family members, neighbors and colleagues search for an explanation of his action.

programme.title: The Chavis Chronicles

programme.title: Between the Covers: Amy Jo Burns

programme.desc: A full-body relaxation workout that stretches and strengthens your feet and calves is showcased.

programme.title: Arthur: Background Blues/ And Now Let's Talk to Some Kids

programme.title: Molly of Denali: Froggy of Denali/ Molly Mabray and the Mystery Stone
programme.desc: When Molly and Tooey find a frog, Molly decides to keep it as a pet, but it turns out their more high maintenance than she thought. Then, Molly and Tooey can't wait to see petroglyphs on their trip to Sitka, but only see water at the beach.

programme.title: Wild Kratts: Archerfish School

programme.title: Hero Elementary: The Crew Who SNOWS What to Do/ Keep Your Eye on the Ball
programme.desc: Turbo Tina is excited for her first snowfall EVER! But, some of the snow melts quickly. Can Sparks' Crew figure out why some snow is melting and some isn't?

programme.desc: Our heroes go back in time to meet Winston Churchill, a person who knew how to command attention!

programme.title: Motorweek

programme.title: Garden Smart

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: They'll Have to Go Through Me

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Assignment of the Year
programme.desc: While Antonio continues his off-duty security job for Asher and Layla, Asher is killed. Voight works the case while making sure Antonio is covered and Halstead and Lindsay go undercover in a rather uncomfortable location.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Called in Dead
programme.desc: A friend of Nadia's leads the intelligence unit to a huge heroin bust. Unfortunately, this bust results in Olinksy arriving home to find his wife being held hostage.

programme.title: Hockey
programme.desc: Semifinal Game 3

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: My Way
programme.desc: Commander Perry notifies the Intelligence Unit that Pulpo has been released to help track down a dangerous cartel leader. Meanwhile, Ruzek gets some difficult news as a blast from Lindsay's past comes back.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: The Docks

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: A Beautiful Friendship

programme.title: Dateline: Unthinkable: The Menendez Murders
programme.desc: The Menendez brothers were convicted more than two decades ago of murdering their parents. Lyle Menendez tells Keith Morrison from behind bars that despite admitting to brutally killing his parents, he believes he should have received a plea deal.

programme.title: Dateline: Unthinkable: The Menendez Murders
programme.desc: The Menendez brothers were convicted more than two decades ago of murdering their parents. Lyle Menendez tells Keith Morrison from behind bars that despite admitting to brutally killing his parents, he believes he should have received a plea deal.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Disrobed
programme.desc: Detectives investigate the unusual circumstances surrounding the murder of an admired judge.

programme.desc: A sexual assault case is re-opened just days before the statute of limitations runs out.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Entitled
programme.desc: The detectives probe the complicated gunshot murder of a believed sexual aggressor.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Assignment of the Year
programme.desc: While Antonio continues his off-duty security job for Asher and Layla, Asher is killed. Voight works the case while making sure Antonio is covered and Halstead and Lindsay go undercover in a rather uncomfortable location.

programme.title: Frasier: Dr. Nora

programme.title: Frasier: When a Man Loves Two Women
programme.desc: Just as Frasier feels good about his present relationship, he meets another woman.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Dateline, Miami
programme.desc: On a Saturday night when Dorothy is the only one with a date, the other girls are prompted to reminisce about some of the worst dates they have ever had.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Monkey Show
programme.desc: A hurricane is brewing, and not just outside, when Stan visits to tell Dorothy he's over her and moving on. Dorothy quickly discovers he's moving on with her sister, Gloria.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Monkey Show
programme.desc: Dorothy's discovery of Gloria sleeping with Stan just adds to their sibling rivalry. Meanwhile, Sophia has had it and leaves just prior the hurricane hitting.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Rose Loves Miles
programme.desc: Rose has had it with Miles' penny pinching ways and decides to take Blanche's advice and go out with her and some big spenders. Meanwhile, Blanche has been left in charge of Sophia while Dorothy is away.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: From Here to the Pharmacy

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Pope's Ring

programme.title: Movie: Mahalia
programme.desc: Danielle Brooks stars in this Robin Roberts produced Lifetime biopic as the gospel legend and civil rights trailblazer.

programme.title: Movie: Salt-N-Pepa
programme.desc: The story of Queensborough Community College students Cheryl 'Salt' James and Sandra 'Pepa' Denton as they fall into the world of rap and hip hop.

programme.title: Movie: Sleeping With the Enemy
programme.desc: A woman tries to escape her abusive husband by staging her death and moving away.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Past Lives: The Madison Rutherford Case
programme.desc: The body of a wealthy American businessman is found in his rental car. When the victim's wife files a claim for $7 million of life insurance, investigators seek the help of a forensic anthropologist and uncover a chilling scheme of fraud and deceit.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Over and Out
programme.desc: A pipe bomb explodes in a house, killing a man and wounding his mother. Police use a lot number on a battery and a mailing label to track down the bomber, and determine his motive.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shot of Vengeance
programme.desc: A 34-year-old nurse, who had been experiencing a variety of flu-like symptoms with no discernible explanation, is diagnosed as HIV-positive, and further investigation reveals that the method in which she contracted the disease was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Dinner and a Movie
programme.desc: A woman is found dead in her home while her husband is on a sailing trip. When investigating, police uncover a lifestyle of sex parties and wife swapping.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Wrong Foot
programme.desc: Illinois police discover a footprint on a birthday card that could lead to the home invader who killed a man and attacked two young girls.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Order Up
programme.desc: The murder of a prominent businessman in Colorado is particularly difficult to solve since the killers left virtually no forensic evidence at the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shear Luck
programme.desc: Investigators have to reassemble a computer disk that has been cut to pieces with shears, in order to determine the guilt or innocence of an Air Force serviceman suspected of brutally murdering his wife.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Tagging a Suspect
programme.desc: Bomb attacks typically pose a difficult problem for investigators to solve due to the absence of the attacker and a damaged crime scene, but forensic scientists manage to uncover miniscule pieces of plastic that lead straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Strong Impressions
programme.desc: The wife of an Air Force officer is found dead in her bed, and authorities initially believe the cause of death to be accidental suffocation by a laundry bag, but further forensic investigation reveals the horrifying truth.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Cereal Killer
programme.desc: Police must search for a boy who goes missing mysteriously after his home burns down and are disheartened when they find his body, but the killer left behind numerous clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Crash Course
programme.desc: A highway patrolman is shocked by a coroner's ruling of an 'accidental death.' after he attends a Crash scene with far more blood than usual. An anonymous tip reinvigorates the investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Fresh Heir
programme.desc: In the 20 years following an unsolved murder, lives changed and so did forensic science. In time, a high-powered microscope and DNA profiling revealed a clue no one had seen before.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Tight-Fitting Genes
programme.desc: A behavioral profile is helpful in a murder investigation, but it's not an answer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Valentine
programme.desc: An obstetrician tried unsuccessfully to revive a man's wife who he found covered in blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Smoke in Your Eyes
programme.desc: The bodies of two young women are found in the ashes of an apartment fire, which appears to have been set deliberately.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Flower Power
programme.desc: Two different men call police to report the same murder. Apparently, neither one knew that the other had called.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Within Arm's Reach
programme.desc: A man accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Private Thoughts
programme.desc: An entire family found dead inside their burned-out home appears to be a murder-suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Death by a Salesman
programme.desc: Even though there were no witnesses, police manage to track down murderer less than 12 hours after the fact. Using fingerprints at the scene and security camera footage, officers arrested the perpetrator even though he was out of the state, on a bus.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Web of Seduction
programme.desc: When the scene of a murder proves to be completely bare of evidence, computer forensics experts manage to dig up files on the victim's computer, and those of his wife and her lover that finger a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files: In Harm's Way
programme.desc: A woman's butchered body is found, and the medical examiner preserves her rib cage bearing a mortal wound and containing the identifying microscopic marks of the blade used; miles away, a knife is found that will be compared to her bone markings.

programme.title: Forensic Files: No Corpus Delecti
programme.desc: A woman disappears without a trace, and even though foul play is suspected the trail goes cold. After twenty years a group of volunteer forensic scientists locate her body and bring the killer to justice.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Breaking News
programme.desc: When a young reporter is murdered in her apartment, and the two suspects are cleared of any wrongdoing, police must turn to a databank of well over 8000 felons to catch the killer, leading to the first conviction in the U.S, based on this evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files: All the World's a Stage
programme.desc: Police must investigate one of their own when a detective says his wife shot herself in the head. While he claims he called 911 as soon as he could, evidence makes police doubt the detective's story.

programme.title: Forensic Files: True Lies
programme.desc: It took a murder victim's 9 year-old child, notations in a diary, and forensic psychology to determine which forensic evidence was important and which to ignore.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Bed of Deceit
programme.desc: When a retired police officer is found shot dead in his bed, investigators suspect suicide. But the evidence, alongside surveillance tape from a nearby bank, seems to indicate it was murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Bitter Pill to Swallow
programme.desc: A pregnant woman experiences severe morning sickness and suspects something is amiss.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sip of Sins
programme.desc: Faryion Wardrip seemed like the model citizen to most, but he was also a brutal serial killer who, for years, eluded police without suspicion. But smart police work and DNA from a discarded paper cup proved to be Wardrip's undoing.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: Wild West Apocalypse

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: Phantoms of Chernobyl

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: Mystery of the Alien Base

programme.title: Mysteries of the Abandoned: Factory of Death

programme.desc: Sparks fly at Elizabeth's family boat outing. Angela is blindsided by disappointing news. Asuelu reveals a traumatic experience. Tiffany's dad makes a big decision. Julia receives important news. Natalie feels insulted by Mike's mom.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Happily Ever After?: More to Love: All Shook Up

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Happily Ever After?: First Look: Not So Silent Partners

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Delivering Baby Mason
programme.desc: After fainting from exhaustion, Kim learns to manage her professional life. Kourt takes Scott back and gives birth to their baby son, Mason.

programme.desc: When Scott's drinking gets out of control, Kourtney refuses to see him until he seeks professional help.

programme.title: Kourtney & Khloé Take Miami: Broken Family
programme.desc: Kourtney asks herself how much she wants Scott in her life after his incident with breaking the mirror.

programme.desc: When Kim moves into her new mansion, Kris insists that she throw a house warming party that grows out of control.

programme.desc: Khloe has a hard time accepting that Rob and her best friend, Malika, are hooking up. Kris must decide whether or not to manage the pop band, BG5.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Only at This Joint

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Coast to Coast Chow

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Barbecue, Batter and Beer Can Chicken
programme.desc: Maui, Hawaii has ridiculous ramen and beer can chicken. Los Angeles has a food truck with a twist on fish and chips. Plus, a barbecue spot with fried chicken.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Griddles n' Vittles
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's headed west for barbecue and beyond. In Los Angeles, a food court gem grills up homemade chorizo and a Yucatan turkey special. Then, in Las Vegas, Nevada, two funky joints fire up the grill.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: From Big Burgers To Little Italy
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri's going from the bomb burgers to a taste of Italy. In Little Rock, Ark., a funky joint pairing their craft brews with all kinds of on-the-bun creations.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Triple D Nation: All Sorts of Seafood
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is diving into all sorts of seafood sensations. In Hawaii, a poke place had to change their address to handle the DDD crowds, and, in San Francisco, a quaint spot is still serving Southern staples with a twist, like their crawfish pot pie.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Cruisin' Capital Cities
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is checking out cuisine in capital cities, starting in Juneau, Alaska, at an Italian spot with an Alaskan twist pairing pappardelle with king crab. In Richmond, a classic deli is cookin' up modern tastes like duck pierogis and Jewish egg rolls.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Poutine, Pizza and Pork
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri travels the country diving into some meaty masterpieces starting at a spot in Longmont, Colorado, that serves craft beer and primo pub grub like their piled-high poutine burger.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Triple D Nation: Meat Lovers
programme.desc: It's meat any way you like it when Guy Fieri heads to Miami to see what his buddy is doing with chicken and duck. A noodle shop in Portland, Ore., is churning out thousands of dumplings a week these days.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Soup, Salad and Seafood

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Paid Program

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics: Cook Like a Pro: Easy High Low
programme.desc: Ina Garten loves mixing fancy and casual fare, and she's sharing her secrets to this winning mashup of high and low. She takes everyday potato and celery root puree over the top with truffle butter and serves it with stunning seared scallops.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics: Cook Like a Pro: Tex-Mex Entertaining
programme.desc: Ina Garten has all the bases covered when it comes to Tex-Mex entertaining. She makes a light and airy tres leches cake with berries and make ahead whipped cream, and her crowd-pleasing pork Posole.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Dishes for Dad
programme.desc: The Kitchen is celebrating dads everywhere pop-pleasing dishes like Sunny's pulled pork eggs benedict and Katie Lee's breakfast sausage eggs and cheeseburgers. Jeff's father, Gus Mauro, stops by to help make cheesesteak tacos. The host share gift ideas.

programme.title: Symon's Dinners Cooking Out: Dad's Sticky Ribs and Decadent Cookies
programme.desc: Michael turns the backyard grill into a smoker to make his dad's favourite sticky ribs. Then, he makes grilled potato salad as his wife turns his dad's obsession with chocolate into giant cookies infuses with chocolate, crunchy pretzel and sea salt.

programme.title: Symon's Dinners Cooking Out: Family-Style Fish Fry
programme.desc: Michael Symon's outdoor, family-friendly meal starts with a fish fry with lime aioli, where he fries fresh fluke in a pot over coals on the grill.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: 16-Minute Meals: Cowboy Favorites
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is sharing 16-minute favorites for cowboys and cowgirls, too. She makes steak medallions and potatoes, gnocchi with chorizo and corn, crisp in a pinch and finally a chicken jalapeno popper quesadilla.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Feeding Now, Feeding Later
programme.desc: Ree makes spicy stewed beef, pimento cheese and bacon grits, roasted broccoli salad and a sour cream and parsley dressing.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Thanks a Brunch, Dad!
programme.desc: The Kitchen serves up a scrumptious brunch that any dad will love. The hosts make Everything Bagel Breakfast Casserole, a Strawberry Pandowdy, Hazelnut Shakerato coffee cocktails and the perfect Father's Day 'mug cake.'

programme.title: Symon's Dinners Cooking Out: Hot and Kinda Fast Brisket
programme.desc: Michael Symon fires up his smoker and firepit to make some of his Dad's and Pap's favorite dishes. He starts with a lesson on how to create the perfect smoked brisket with his pastrami rubbed brisket.

programme.title: Guy's Ranch Kitchen: Entertaining: Steakhouse at Home
programme.desc: Restaurant fads come and go, but authentic and time-honored steakhouse classics are never out of style. Guy Fieri invites his chef friends to the ranch for an evening of entertaining and steakhouse favorites.

programme.title: Chopped: Grill Masters Napa: Part One
programme.desc: Sixteen chefs are fired up to grill in beautiful Napa Valley, Calif. for another epic tournament. There's $10,000 at stake in each preliminary competition, and the champs will fight it out for $50,000 more.

programme.title: Chopped: Grill Masters Napa: Part Two
programme.desc: Baked beans and lamb belly are two must-use ingredients in the first round, and in the entree round, the grill masters are given a seafood-heavy basket.

programme.title: Chopped: Grill Masters Napa: Part Three
programme.desc: Another group of grillers get a chance to compete in California's beautiful Napa Valley! The grill masters pound away in the first round in an attempt to tenderize the basket's seafood protein.

programme.title: Chopped: Grill Masters Napa: Part Four
programme.desc: Three grilling champs have already won a spot in the grand finale, and now a new group furiously competes for the final spot. In the appetizer round, the grill masters face some beautiful mushrooms and strong jam.

programme.title: The Goldbergs: We Didn't Start the Fire

programme.title: The Goldbergs: The Goldberg Girls

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Playdate With Destiny

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Women 'N Tuition
programme.desc: Christine tries to make some sacrifices in order to pay for Ritchie's rising school tuition. Meanwhile, Matthew realizes he hasn't been living up to his potential and considers some extreme changes.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Mission: Impossible
programme.desc: Annoyed that Ritchie finds his weekends with his dad more fun than his weekdays with her, Christine decides to make Richard responsible for having Ritchie complete his big homework assignment.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: What About Barb?
programme.desc: When Barb starts working at Christine's gym, which she recently bought into, Christine can't accept the reality of not being in complete control. Meanwhile, Matthew helps Ritchie find the perfect nanny to replace him when he starts medical school.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Crash
programme.desc: When Christine's environmentally friendly hybrid car and Marly's gas-guzzling monster SUV hit one another, it becomes a bigger dispute than simply who hit whom. While her car is in the shop, Christine has to rent an SUV when no other car is available.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Ritchie Scores
programme.desc: Christine reluctantly signs up Ritchie for soccer after Mr. Harris informs her and Richard that he's had trouble making friends. Christine claims not to believe in competitive sports but finds herself in competition with Richard for Mr. Harris' attention.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Endless Shrimp, Endless Night
programme.desc: Christine attempts to re-ignite the relationship between her brother Matthew and his ex-girlfriend. Meanwhile, Richard tries to get Ritchie interested in his job as a contractor.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Blanche's Little Girl

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Dorothy's New Friend
programme.desc: Dorothy befriends a novelist, Barbara Thorndyke, and she quickly infringes on Dorothy's relationship with Blanche and Rose, who think Barbara is nothing but a stuck up snob.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Grab That Dough

programme.title: The Golden Girls: My Brother My Father
programme.desc: Sophia's brother, Angelo, a priest, is coming to Miami for a visit and he doesn't know Dorothy and Stan are divorced. So in order to spare his feelings, Sophia convinces Dorothy and Stan to pretend to be married.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: And Ma Makes Three
programme.desc: Feeling sorry for a lonely Sophia, Dorothy allows her to come along on a date with her and Dorothy's latest beau. However, pretty soon Sophia begins tagging along on every date which puts a rift in Dorothy's romantic plans.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Larceny and Old Lace

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Rose's Big Adventure
programme.desc: Rose's latest boyfriend has just retired and she is ready to spend a whole lot more time with him, but his retirement quickly turns him into a couch potato. Meanwhile, the girls deal with remodeling the garage into a guest room.

programme.desc: Polka, a bottlenose dolphin, has a raspy voice and needs to see the doctor to get the all-clear. Staff excitedly prepare for the upcoming puffin breeding season. Two woebegone sharks are due for a checkup. A curious and active box turtle goes for a walk.

programme.desc: Technology from California is brought to the aquarium to monitor beluga whale activity. Maggie the Asian small-clawed otter's training kicks in when receiving a shot, and Morty the sea star undergoes a checkup after suspicious growths form.

programme.desc: Steve Irwin's family carry on his legacy as they run the Australian Zoo.

programme.title: My Lottery Dream Home: Almost Throwing Away a Million

programme.title: My Lottery Dream Home: Million-Dollar Baby
programme.desc: Thanks to a million-dollar win on a scratch-off, a couple and their baby can finally move out of their one-bedroom apartment. David is happy to help this family find a bigger home near Binghamton, New York, even if he does struggle with the city's name.

programme.title: My Lottery Dream Home: The Great American Dream Home
programme.desc: Brenda and Jesse were living in a tiny apartment in Columbus, Ohio, when they won a million-dollar lottery. Now, David Bromstad takes them on an emotional and memorable journey to help them find their American dream home.

programme.title: 100 Day Dream Home: Building a New Life Together
programme.desc: Two engaged police officers have been living in a cramped trailer on rural land they have been trying to clear themselves. Brian and Mika take over the heavy equipment and build this couple's dream home so that they can actually plan their wedding!

programme.title: 100 Day Dream Home: Big Move From the Big Apple

programme.title: 100 Day Dream Home: Brian and Mika Dream Big

programme.title: Man v. Food: Puerto Rico

programme.title: Man v. Food: Richmond

programme.title: Man v. Food: Long Island

programme.title: Man v. Food: Oklahoma City
programme.desc: Adam Richman is in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma for a presidential t-bone steak, the Sooner State original, fried onion burger and the Fried Catfish Challenge at the Steak and Catfish Barn.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Kansas City
programme.desc: Adam Richman is in Kansas City, Missouri for some world-class barbecue, some of the best pan-fried chicken you'll find anywhere and the six-pound Ultimate Destroyer sandwich at Papa Bob's Bar-B-Que.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Indianapolis
programme.desc: Adam Richman is in Indianapolis, Indiana for the Indy staple pork tenderloin sandwich, some Hoosier home cooking and the enormous Big Ugly Burger Challenge at Bub's Burgers and Ice Cream.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Jersey Shore
programme.desc: Adam Richman's at the famous Jersey Shore to try a Jersey-original-Trenton style tomato pie plus a colossal octopus shaped ice cream sundae and the insanely spicy Ludicrous Wings Challenge at Chicken or the Egg.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Syracuse

programme.title: Man v. Food: Niagara Falls
programme.desc: Adam is in Niagara Falls, the honeymoon capital of the world. Adam has a date with a steak you can barely fit on a plate at the Riverstone Grill. Then he crosses the border for the Italian Challenge at Mick and Angelo's.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence: UFOs and Temples of Gold
programme.desc: UFO experts investigate whether the value, reverence and desire for gold is the result of extraterrestrial landings in the ancient past.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence: UFO Abduction
programme.desc: Only recently have researchers begun to understand and investigate the phenomenon of alien abduction. While today such claims are dismissed as hoax or delusion, there are time-tested, landmark cases that mainstream scholars struggle to refute.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence: Police UFO Files
programme.desc: Using archival audio recordings, official transcripts and incident reports, UFO researchers investigate what really happened during incredible and sometimes life-threatening encounters between law enforcement and UFOs.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence: Nazi UFO Secrets
programme.desc: Shocking evidence reveals the development of secret Nazi weapons not long after an alleged UFO crashed in Germany's Black Forest. UFO experts investigate whether the US government co-opted this technology for our own space and military programs.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence: American UFO Coverups
programme.desc: Recent news of the Pentagon’s secret UFO program may lead to shocking evidence of what the military has hidden for decades.

programme.title: Shahs of Sunset: The Golden Years
programme.desc: Persian-American living a life of luxury try balancing social life, career and family.

programme.title: Shahs of Sunset: Big Little Persian Lies
programme.desc: Temperatures continue to rise on Mike’s birthday trip to Palm Springs. From ponies to fire dancers, the crew pulls out all the stops. With Paulina on her way, however, it’s only a matter of time before tensions come to a boil.

programme.title: Shahs of Sunset: The Truth Shall Sext you Free

programme.title: Mastermind of Murder: The Will to Kill

programme.title: Black-ish: Juneteenth
programme.desc: The Johnsons go to Jack and Diane's school play about Columbus Day, and Dre is dismayed by the historically inaccurate way that the holiday is portrayed.

programme.title: Black-ish: Hope
programme.desc: When the kids ask some tough questions in the midst of a highly publicized court case involving alleged police brutality and an African-American teenager, Dre and Bow are conflicted on how best to field them.

programme.title: Black-ish: Martin Luther Skiing Day
programme.desc: On the way to the Johnson family's annual Martin Luther King holiday ski trip, Dre realizes his children may not know the significance of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. so he decides to inundate them with Dr. King history facts.

programme.title: Black-ish: THE Word
programme.desc: Jack performs the song 'Gold Digger' at a school talent show and when he sings a lyric that includes THE word, it leads to his possible expulsion from school.

programme.title: Black-ish: Elephant in the Room
programme.desc: Liberals Dre and Bow go ballistic when Junior declares he's joined the Young Republican Club to impress a girl (ironically named Hillary). Meanwhile, everyone teases Zoey when she finds out she needs glasses.

programme.title: Black-ish: Switch Hitting
programme.desc: When super cool and white exec Jay Sloane challenges Dre over his street cred and picks inept Charlie for a big urban market account, Dre finds himself questioning his 'blackness.' Pops gets a letter from the IRS and fears the worst.

programme.title: Black-ish: Black Like Us
programme.desc: Dre and Bow are furious after Diane isn't lit properly in her class photo. Diane doesn't want to make it a big deal, but they decide to talk to the principal about the need for sensitivity toward all complexions.

programme.title: Black-ish: Mad and Boujee
programme.desc: When Dre is invited to join a private social club for the black upper class, Bow and the family jump at the opportunity to mingle with this new community, but Dre’s not so sure these exclusive clubs are all they’re cracked up to be.

programme.title: Black-ish: Hair Day
programme.desc: Bow takes Diane to get her hair done at the salon and wants to spend this time together as a bonding opportunity, but Diane isn't having it. Diane is conflicted about relaxing her hair again.

programme.title: Black-ish: Sink or Swim
programme.desc: After Dre notices that his neighbour Janine has never invited his family to any of her pool parties, he assumes she is racially stereotyping them as a family that doesn't swim. The kids are shocked to find out that, in fact, Dre doesn’t know how to swim.

programme.title: Black-ish: Good in the 'Hood
programme.desc: Dre's sister Rhonda takes Jack and Diane to her part of town in a black neighbourhood and claims the twins are scared of their own community. Dre and Rhonda take them to a local diner to show them why it's so special.

programme.title: Black-ish: One Angry Man
programme.desc: Dre is forced to participate in jury duty after Junior responds to the summons that was thrown away. The case is supposed to be open and shut, but when Dre sees that the defendant is a young African American, he feels responsible to give him a fair trial.

programme.title: Black-ish: ToysRn'tUs
programme.desc: Janine gives Diane a white Girlstory doll for her birthday, and when Bow tries to return it for a black doll, she is shocked by the limited options offered.

programme.title: Black-ish: Who's Afraid of the Big Black Man
programme.desc: After a situation arises at work, Dre's views are confronted regarding how black people are perceived now versus when he grew up. He wants to be more open-minded about being a part of his community, so he joins the Homeowners' Association.

programme.title: Black-ish: Bow Knows
programme.desc: Dre is selected to run point on a campaign that focuses on 'The Talk,' which refers to the conversations that Black parents have with their children through the decades to prepare them for the racial bias they may face in the world.

programme.title: Black-ish: Lemons
programme.desc: The nation is still reeling after the outcome of the presidential election, and tensions are particularly high at Dre's work. Much to his frustration, they haven't been able to get anything done since election night eight weeks ago.

programme.title: Black-ish: Black History Month
programme.desc: Dre is upset after he learns that Jack and Diane are getting the same surface-level education about Black History Month that he got when he was growing up.

programme.title: Black-ish: Juneteenth
programme.desc: The Johnsons go to Jack and Diane's school play about Columbus Day, and Dre is dismayed by the historically inaccurate way that the holiday is portrayed.

programme.title: Movie: The Karate Kid
programme.desc: A displaced and naïve teenager is taught karate in order to defend himself against trained bullies.

programme.title: Chimps of the Lost Gorge
programme.desc: A family lives in a deep, ancient forested gorge in Uganda, that runs through Africa's savannah, of which has been cut off from the rest of the jungle for 15 years.

programme.title: Hidden Habitats: Svalbard
programme.desc: A Look at the archipelago of Svalbard in the Arctic Circle. Polar bears, ringed seals, sea birds and reindeer all feature in the food web but the ecosystem is driven by the producers at the bottom of the food chain.

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth: January-February: The Year Begins
programme.desc: As the Earth's axial tilt brings a scorching summer to some places and frigid winter to others, life must adapt to the extreme conditions.

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth: March-April: A Time of Renewal
programme.desc: As Spring is sprung cherry blossoms bloom across Japan and the final raft of green sea turtles arrive at the Great Barrier Reef to lay their eggs.

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth: May-June: A Time of Volatility
programme.desc: As the summer unfolds in the Northern Hemisphere some regions enter their wet season, and some go dry which creates a scarcity for some and bounty for others.

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth: July-August: A Time of Migration
programme.desc: With the Arctic ice sheets falling, and the summer melt beginning July brings dramatic natural shifts.

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth: September-October: A Time of Activation
programme.desc: As the Northern Hemisphere turns to autumn, Australia comes back to life and the mating humpbacks fill the warming tropical waters of Tonga.

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth: November-December: Full Circle
programme.desc: From the millions of Monarch butterflies that collect in Mexico, to the deep fog of Dubai, November brings beautiful signals of winter.

programme.title: Planet Earth: Enchanted Kingdom
programme.desc: Narrated by Idris Elba, explore seven distinct worlds in the wildest continent on Earth, Africa, to discover that nature is so much more surprising and extraordinary than ever imagined.

programme.title: Planet Earth: Ice Worlds
programme.desc: The Arctic and Antarctic experience the most extreme seasons on Earth. Time-lapse cameras watch a colony of penguins, while aerial images show a polar bear swimming more than 100 km.

programme.title: Planet Earth: Great Plains
programme.desc: After filming for three years, the shy Mongolian gazelle and the bizarre-looking Tibetan fox are captured on film. Over six weeks, the team follows a pride of 30 lions as they attempt to hunt elephants.

programme.title: Planet Earth: Jungles
programme.desc: Jungles cover roughly three per cent of our planet yet contain 50 per cent of the world's species. In the Ngogo forest, the largest chimpanzee group defends its territory from neighbouring groups. Other specialists include a parasitic fungi.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: The Revolutionaries
programme.desc: Reagan's business mandate leads to a new breed of entrepreneurs, including Steve Jobs, whose '1984' ad helped launch the Super Bowl commercial craze and Ted Turner, who invented the 24-hour news network.

programme.title: The '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Shop 'Til You Drop
programme.desc: Glamour finds a new poster girl in the 1980s, when a young singer is launched to stardom with a shocking performance of 'Like a Virgin' at the first MTV Music Video Awards inspiring girls across the globe to hit the mall.

programme.title: '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Masters of the Universe
programme.desc: Traders on Wall Street mirror the movie with insider trading and drug use. CEOs see their salaries skyrocket while women try to overcome the boys' clubs. NASA's space shuttle Challenger explodes during takeoff.

programme.title: '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Tear Down These Walls
programme.desc: The 80s was known for greed but it also marked the alliance between charity and celebrity. Huge events are staged to deal with the crisis of poverty. Reagan is breaking down barriers with this foreign policy as he pushes to end the Cold War.

programme.title: Movie: Rise Again: Tulsa and the Red Summer
programme.desc: The discovery of a mass grave in Tulsa Oklahoma begins to unearth the hidden history of racial violence in America.

programme.title: Movie: Rise Again: Tulsa and the Red Summer
programme.desc: The discovery of a mass grave in Tulsa Oklahoma begins to unearth the hidden history of racial violence in America.

programme.title: '80s: The Decade That Made Us: Super Power
programme.desc: America deploys a powerful 'weapon' to hasten the fall of the Berlin Wall: pop culture. American movies, TV shows, music, and even the news, carry U.S. values and ideas out to the world and into the Soviet Bloc, swaying hearts and minds.

programme.title: Bloods and Crips: L.A. Gangs
programme.desc: NGC takes viewers to the streets of South Central Los Angeles as it charts the rise of two of the most notorious gangs in America: the Bloods and the Crips.

programme.desc: Enter the intersecting worlds of immigration and gang violence and follow the trail of a murder from Greensboro, North Carolina to El Salvador, going behind the scenes in America's war on transnational gangs and its unintended consequences.
